subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the poem’s imagery, sound, and mood in depth
Interpret the poet’s use of figurative language, symbolism, and rhythm
Explore philosophical and emotional reflections evoked by rain
Develop critical thinking and analytical writing skills
Connect poetic themes to personal experiences and universal human feelings

lesson_plan.title: Introduction and Poet Context
topics_covered: Historical and literary context of the poem
Theme of reflection, nostalgia, and human connection with nature
teaching_strategy: Lecture on poet’s life and style
Discuss cultural and historical relevance of nature poetry
student_activities: Take notes on poet and context
Discuss initial impressions of rain and human emotions

lesson_plan.title: Stanza Analysis: Literal and Symbolic Meaning
topics_covered: Line-by-line analysis of first half of poem
Literal description of rain and roof
Symbolism of rain for reflection and memory
teaching_strategy: Detailed explanation of imagery and symbols
Encourage students to visualize scenes and emotions
student_activities: Annotate stanzas with notes on literal and symbolic meaning
Explain personal interpretation of each stanza

lesson_plan.title: Figurative Language and Poetic Devices
topics_covered: How rhythm and sound reinforce mood
Connection of devices to thematic expression
teaching_strategy: Highlight devices in text and analyze their impact
Use examples from the poem to illustrate effect
student_activities: Write notes on how each enhances meaning

lesson_plan.title: Emotional and Philosophical Reflection
topics_covered: Reflection on nostalgia and human memory
Interpretation of rain as metaphor for life’s continuity
Exploration of inner thoughts evoked by nature
teaching_strategy: Guide students to connect poem to personal life experiences
student_activities: Write reflective paragraph on feelings evoked by rain

lesson_plan.title: Critical Analysis and Comparative Discussion
topics_covered: Compare poem with other nature poems
Discussion on universality of human emotions evoked by rain
student_activities: Compare with one other poem of similar theme

lesson_plan.title: Revision, Analytical Summary, and Assessment
topics_covered: Recap of all stanzas, devices, mood, and themes
Discussion of abstract meanings and life lessons
teaching_strategy: Short essay writing on thematic and stylistic analysis
student_activities: Write analytical essay connecting imagery, mood, and theme
Participate in class discussion and peer feedback

teaching_aids: Charts showing figurative devices, imagery, and rhythm
Handouts with reflection and analysis questions
evaluation_methods: Short written tests on stanza meanings and devices
teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyze both literal and symbolic meanings, linking imagery, sound, and rhythm to mood and philosophical reflection on life and memory.
